documentation.readme: page-fetch is a tool for researchers that lets you:
usage_sections: Installation: page-fetch is written with Go and can be installed with go install: Or you can clone the respository and build it manually:
Options: You can get the page-fetch help output by running page-fetch -h:

arguments.name: -e
arguments.description: Do not save responses matching the provided string (can be specified multiple times)

arguments.name: --exclude
arguments.description: Do not save responses matching the provided string (can be specified multiple times)

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Only save requests matching the provided string (can be specified multiple times)

arguments.name: --include
arguments.description: Only save requests matching the provided string (can be specified multiple times)

arguments.name: --no-third-party
arguments.description: Do not save responses to requests on third-party domains

arguments.name: --overwrite
arguments.description: Overwrite output files when they already exist

arguments.name: --third-party
arguments.description: Only save responses to requests on third-party domains

arguments.name: -w
arguments.description: Overwrite output files when they already exist

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zmap/zmap (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: page-fetch is a tool for researchers that lets you:.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: page-fetch is a tool for researchers that lets you:.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `GET $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL 200 text/html; charset=utf-8`. Further invocations from upstream: `GET $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL 200 text/css`; `GET $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL 200 image/svg+xml`. page-fetch is a tool for researchers that lets you:.

name: page-fetch
summary: page-fetch is a tool for researchers that lets you:

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need web, use page-fetch because page-fetch is a tool for researchers that lets you:.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `GET $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL 200 text/html; charset=utf-8` as documented upstream for page-fetch.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `GET $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL 200 text/css` as documented upstream for page-fetch.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `GET $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL 200 image/svg+xml` as documented upstream for page-fetch.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `GET $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL 200 text/css; charset=utf-8` as documented upstream for page-fetch.
When chaining web tools, pair page-fetch with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `page-fetch --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
